rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes a strong contribution claim ('first convex optimization framework', 'first principled approach'); improving the draft requires verifying that the thesis and gap statement are precise and defensible.
rationale.R1.2: The draft contains technical mathematical claims (Bregman projection, O(n log m) complexity, O(m log T / T) regret), internal consistency of premises-to-conclusion chains is central to the draft's credibility and must be checked during improvement.
rationale.R1.3: Three benchmark results (Spearman ρ = 0.74, 0.71, 0.69; 6–9 pp and 2–4 pp deltas) are offered as direct support for the optimality and superiority claims; alignment between these numbers and the stated claims is a core improvement target.
rationale.R1.4: The draft asserts primacy twice ('first convex…', 'first principled…'); improving the draft requires assessing whether those novelty claims hold against the cited and uncited prior-work landscape.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity is the most natural load-bearing expectation of 'improve this draft'; the prose contains dense notation-heavy passages that require polish for readability and flow.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus primarily on prose clarity and writing quality (R1.6 dominant), or do you also want a technical review that checks mathematical correctness, novelty claims, and evidence-claim alignment (R1.2, R1.3, R1.4 co-dominant)?
